title: Apple-Cinnamon Breakfast Pastry
cScaleSummary: C0 because the recipe contains no cholesterol-contributing animal-derived ingredients. The apples, dates, flour, olive oil, applesauce, oat milk, nuts, cider, lemon, and spices contribute 0 mg cholesterol.
ldlActiveSummary: LDL Active because the pastry is cholesterol-free, uses fruit and dates for sweetness, includes whole-grain flour, and uses unsaturated fat from olive oil and nuts rather than butter, cream, shortening, or saturated animal fat.
intro: Apple-cinnamon breakfast pastry sits in the familiar lane of hand pies, turnovers, and morning bakery cases, but this version keeps the structure lean and breakfast-friendly. The sweetness comes from apples, Medjool dates, and reduced apple cider, while cinnamon, lemon, vanilla, and a crisp-edged dough make it feel like a pastry instead of a compromise.

stepGroups.title: Make the Apple-Date Filling
items: Combine {{quantity:apples}} apples, {{quantity:medjool-dates}} Medjool dates, {{quantity:apple-cider-filling}} apple cider, {{quantity:lemon-juice}} lemon juice, {{quantity:lemon-zest}} lemon zest, {{quantity:cinnamon}} cinnamon, {{quantity:vanilla-extract}} vanilla extract, and {{quantity:kosher-salt-filling}} kosher salt in a small saucepan.
Cook over medium heat for 8 to 10 minutes, stirring often, until the apples soften and the dates break down into a sticky, jammy coating.
Mash about one-third of the apples against the side of the pan with a spoon. The filling should be thick, glossy, and spoonable, not wet.
Stir in {{quantity:walnuts}} walnuts.
Spread the filling on a plate and cool completely before assembling the pastries. Warm filling will soften the dough and make the pastries harder to seal.

stepGroups.title: Reduce the Apple Cider
items: Pour {{quantity:apple-cider-glaze}} apple cider into a small saucepan.
Simmer over medium heat for 8 to 12 minutes, until reduced to about 2 tablespoons.
The cider should be syrupy enough to lightly coat a spoon, but not thick like caramel. Set aside.

stepGroups.title: Make the Pastry Dough
items: Whisk together {{quantity:white-whole-wheat-flour}} white whole wheat flour, {{quantity:all-purpose-flour}} all-purpose flour, {{quantity:baking-powder}} baking powder, and {{quantity:kosher-salt-dough}} kosher salt in a mixing bowl.
Add {{quantity:olive-oil}} olive oil and {{quantity:unsweetened-applesauce}} unsweetened applesauce.
Mix with a fork until the flour looks evenly moistened and slightly pebbly.
Add {{quantity:oat-milk}} cold unsweetened oat milk and mix gently, stopping as soon as the dough comes together.
Turn the dough onto a surface dusted with {{quantity:bench-flour}} all-purpose flour and knead 5 or 6 times, just until smooth.
Shape the dough into a disk, cover, and rest for 15 minutes.

stepGroups.title: Assemble the Pastries
items: Heat the oven to 400°F and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Roll the rested dough into a thin rectangle, about 1/8 inch thick, using the remaining bench flour only as needed to prevent sticking.
Trim the edges, then cut the dough into 6 roughly equal rectangles.
Place one-sixth of the cooled apple-date filling on one half of each rectangle, leaving a clean border around the edges.
Fold the dough over the filling to make pockets.
Press the edges firmly with your fingers, then crimp with a fork.
Cut 2 small vents in the top of each pastry.
Brush the tops lightly with the reduced apple cider.

stepGroups.title: Bake and Finish
items: Bake the pastries at 400°F for 18 to 22 minutes, until the edges are browned and the tops are firm and lightly golden.
Brush the hot pastries with the remaining reduced apple cider as soon as they come out of the oven.
Let the pastries rest for 10 minutes before serving so the filling settles and the crust firms slightly.

stepGroups.title: Make-Ahead Freezer Method
items: To freeze before baking, assemble and vent the pastries but do not brush them with reduced apple cider.
Freeze the unbaked pastries flat on parchment until solid, then vacuum seal gently or seal in an airtight freezer bag.
Bake frozen pastries directly from the freezer at 400°F for 24 to 30 minutes, brushing with reduced apple cider before baking and again after baking.
